author: jameshe5018
content:
I want to revisit [ethereum/consensus-specs#4777 (comment) ](https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/4777#discussion_r2634788761) that <@586161934425128960>  brought up about proposer preferences. 

ideally we use a newly introduced proposer preferences endpoint to replace `eth/v1/validator/prepare_beacon_proposer ` and `/eth/v1/validator/register_validator` 
the data is esentially the same... 

the problem is by the protocol spec proposer preferences can only be sent out for the next epoch. 

this causes a problem ( as stated in nico's comments) that if your node retstarts after being offline for an epoch or if you're unlucky with a new validator activating and needing to propose in the same epoch. your proposer preferences won't go through at all. with the old endpoints this was never the case, you could add new validators or restart validators and it would at least attempt to get it updated in time for your proposal

author: jameshe5018
content: this also triggers when adding new validators ( whether through migrations or otherwise) on the same edge case

author: nflaig
content: yes, I think for the local caches we definitley need to support current epoch, ideally same for p2p
